title: cissus natalitia
intro: Cissus natalitia, a species of flowering plant in the grape family native to southern Africa, is also known as the Natal vine or Bushman's glue. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Cissus natalitia are attributed to its ability to improve bone density, reduce joint pain, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal remedy by infusing its leaves and stems in hot water or using them in a topical application, and it has also been used as a natural adhesive.
